1000 WILSON BLVD.

© COPYRIGHT 2012 AEROSPACE INDUSTRIES ASSOCIATION OF AMERICA, INC. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

TABLE II - OFFSET CRUCIFORM RECESS DIMENSIONS

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

TABLE III - DOVETAIL SLOT RECESS DIMENSIONS

| DIA             | RECESS   |            |                   | K             |            | RECESS MIN         |
|-----------------|----------|------------|-------------------|---------------|------------|--------------------|
| DASH &#124; NO. | NO.      |            | MIN&#124; MAX REF |               |            | REF TORQUE IN LBS. |
| 3               |          |            | .036.039          | 1.224         | 100        | 37                 |
| 4               |          | .042&#124; | L.045             | .300%         | 110        | 50                 |
| 5               | 4        |            |                   | .046.049.394. | 134        | 125                |
|                 | 5        | . 061      | .064              | .484&#124;    | .160       | 250                |
| 7B              | vo       | . 066      | .070              | 1.571         | &#124;.198 | 430                |
| 8               |          | 082        | .086              | 660%          | .224       | 760                |
| 9               | 8        | . 094      | .098              | .731          | .256       | 1,060              |
| 10              | 9        | 100        | &#124;.104.819    |               | .300       | 1,440              |

MATERIAL:

ALLOY STEEL: 4140 (UNS G41400) PER AMS6382, 4340 (UNS G43406) PER AMS6415 OR AMS6484, OR 8740 (UNS G87400) PER AMS6322.

HEAT TREAT:

FINISH:

CRES:

A286 (UNS S66286) SHALL CONFORM TO THE CHEMISTRY OF AM55731, AMS5732, AMS5737 OR AMS5853.

TITANIUM ALLOY:

6AL-4V (UNS R56400) PER AMS4928 OR AMS4967.

DEVELOP BASIC MATERIAL PROPERTIES AS FOLLOWS, WITH CONTROLS PER AMS2759 FOR ALLOY STEEL AND CRES AND AMS2801 FOR TITANIUM.

ALLOY STEEL:

160-180 KSI FTU

CRES:

160-190 KI FU (9)

TITANIUM:

160 KSI FTU MIN

ALLOY STEEL: CADMIUM PLATE PER AMS-QQ-P-416, TYPE II, CLASS 2.

CRES:

PASSIVATE IN ACCORDANCE WITH AMS2700 METHOD 1, TYPE 2 OR 8, CLASS 1 OR CADMIUM PLATE PER AMS-QQ-P-416, TYPE II, CLASS 2. (SEE CODE FOR FINISH)

TITANIUM:

NONE OR ALUMINUM COATING PER NAS4006. (SEE CODE FOR FINISH)

REVISION

12

NAS1581

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

## NATIONAL AEROSPACE STANDARD

© COPYRIGHT 2012 AEROSPACE INDUSTRIES ASSOCIATION OF AMERICA, INC. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

NATIONAI AEROSPACE STANDARDS

CODE:

THE DIAMETER DASH NUMBER FOLLOWING THE MATERIAL CODE DESIGNATES THE NOMINAL DIAMETER IN .0625 INCH INCREMENTS AND THE GRIP DASH NUMBER FOLLOWING THE RECESS CODE DESIGNATES THE GRIP LENGTH IN .0625 INCH INCREMENTS. IF MORE THAN ONE CODE LETTER IS USED IN THE PART NUMBER FOLLOWING THE GRIP DASH NUMBER, ARRANGE THE CODE LETTERS ALPHABETICALLY.

## MATERIAL:

FINISH:

CODE LETTER "A" FOLLOWING BASIC PART NUMBER INDICATES ALLOY STEEL. (8) CODE LETTER "F" FOLLOWING BASIC PART NUMBER INDICATES ALLOY STEEL. CODE LETTER "C" FOLLOWING BASIC PART NUMBER INDICATES CRES. CODE LETTER "V" FOLLOWING BASIC PART NUMBER INDICATES TITANIUM 6AL-4V. (8) CODE LETTER "K" FOLLOWING BASIC PART NUMBER INDICATES TITANIUM.

ALLOY STEEL: NO CODE LETTER FOLLOWING DIAMETER DASH NUMBER INDICATES CADMIUM PLATE.

RECESS:

CRES:

NO CODE LETTER FOLLOWING GRIP DASH NUMBER INDICATES PASSIVATE. CODE LETTER "P" FOLLOWING GRIP DASH NUMBER INDICATES CADMIUM PLATE.

TITANIUM:

NO CODE FOLLOWING GRIP DASH NUMBER INDICATES NO FINISH. CODE LETTER "G" FOLLOWING GRIP DASH NUMBER INDICATES ALUMINUM COAT.

CODE LETTER "H" FOLLOWING DIA DASH NUMBER INDICATES DOVETAIL SLOT RECESS PER NASM33750.

CODE LETTER "R" FOLLOWING DIA DASH NUMBER INDICATES RIBBED OFFSET CRUCIFORM RECESS PER NASM14191 FOR DIA DASH NUMBERS 3, 4 AND 5. CODE LETTER "T" FOLLOWING DIA DASH NUMBER INDICATES OFFSET CRUCIFORM RECESS PER NASM33781.

CODE LETTER "X" FOLLOWING THE GRIP DASH NUMBER INDICATES REPLACEMENT OVERSIZE REPAIR BOLT. (SEE SHEET 6)

OVERSIZE:

## EXAMPLE OF PART NUMBER:

NAS1581C4H5 =.2500-28 CRES BOLT, 312 GRIP LENGTH, PASSIVATED, NASM33750 DOVETAIL SLOT RECESS.

NAS1581C4T5P = 2500-28 CRES BOLT, .312 GRIP LENGTH, CADMIUM PLATE, NASM33781 OFFSET CRUCIFORM RECESS

NAS1581V4R5 = 2500-28 TITANIUM BOLT, 312 GRIP LENGTH, NASM14191 RIBBED OFFSET CRUCIFORM RECESS.

NAS1581F4H5 = 2500-28 ALLOY STEEL BOLT, .312 GRIP LENGTH, CADMIUM PLATE, NASM33750 DOVETAIL SLOT RECESS.

NAS1581K4R5 = .2500-28 TITANIUM BOLT, 312 GRIP LENGTH, NASM14191 RIBBED OFFSET CRUCIFORM RECESS.

NAS1581K4T5G = .2500-28 TITANIUM BOLT, .312 GRIP LENGTH, NASM33781 OFFSET CRUCIFORM RECESS, ALUMINUM COATING.

## NOTES:

- (1) CONCENTRICITY: CONICAL SURFACE OF THE HEAD TOØD SHALL BE WITHIN .003 FIM. ØD TO THREAD PITCH DIAMETER SHALL BE WITHIN .0045 FIM FOR DIA DASH NUMBER -3 THRU -5, AND SHALL BE WITHIN .006 FIM FOR DIA DASH
- (2) SURFACE TEXTURE: SEE PROCUREMENT SPECIFICATION.

NUMBER -6 AND LARGER.

- (4) BREAK SHARP EDGES AND REMOVE ALL BURRS.
- (3) DELETED.

REVISION

12

NAS1581

SHEET 3

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

## NATIONAL AEROSPACE STANDARD

© COPYRIGHT 2012 AEROSPACE INDUSTRIES ASSOCIATION OF AMERICA, INC. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

- (5) DIMENSIONS IN INCHES AND SHALL BE MET AFTER FINISH, IF APPLICABLE
- (6) NASM14191 RECESS APPLICABLE TO DIA DASH NUMBERS 3, 4 AND 5. NASM33781 AND NASM33750 RECESS APPLICABLE TO ALL DIA DASH NUMBERS.
3. (7)
- (9) FOR CRES ONLY, THE EFFECT OF COLD WORK AND AGING INDUCED DURING THE MANUFACTURING CYCLE MAY INCREASE THE ULTIMATE TENSILE STRENGTH OF THE FINISHED PART, BUT ANY PART (TYPE I, II OR III AS LISTED IN NAS4003) SHALL NOT EXCEED 1.3 TIMES THE SPECIFIED TYPE I MINIMUM TENSILE VALUES AS LISTED IN NAS4003.
- (8) MATERIAL CODES "A" AND "V" CANCELLED AFTER DECEMBER 1991. SEE "F", "K", OR "C" CODES. "F" AND "K" CODES REPRESENT REDUCED TENSILE VALUES BASED ON HEAD STRENGTH. FOR DESCRIPTION OF STATUS NOTES SEE NAS380.
- (10) FLUSH HEAD PROTRUSION VALUES AND GAGING METHOD PER NAS9800.
- (12) ØA, E, AND H ARE INCLUDED FOR ENGINEERING REFERENCE PURPOSES ONLY AND ARE NOT TO BE USED FOR INSPECTION. VALUES ØA, E AND H ARE CALCULATED LIMITS RESULTING FROM THE TOLERANCE ON ØD, HEAD ANGLE AND THE APPLICABLE HEAD PROTRUSION AND GAGE DIAMETER AS DEFINED IN NAS9800.
- (11) GRIP LENGTH SHALL BE MEASURED FROM THE TOP OF BOLT HEAD TO THE END OF THE FULL CYLINDRICAL PORTION OF THE SHANK.
9. HEAD EDGE OUT OF ROUNDNESS SHALL NOT EXCEED "Y" FIM.
- (15) POINT SHALL BE FLAT AND CHAMFERED. CHAMFER PLUS INCOMPLETE THREAD SHALL NOT
- (14) SHANK SHALL BE STRAIGHT WITHIN "Z" VALUES FIM PER INCH OF LENGTH.
- (16) PER APPLICABLE PROCUREMENT SPECIFICATION, THE BOLT RECESS SHALL. BE TORQUE TESTED IN BOTH INSTALLATION AND REMOVAL DIRECTION WITH APPLICABLE NASM33781, NASM14191 OR NASM33750 DRIVER WITH AXIAL END PRESSURE NOT EXCEEDING 15 POUNDS. BOLTS ARE REJECTABLE IF MINIMUM TORQUE VALUES LISTED BELOW CAUSE FRACTURE OF BOLT OR DISTORTION WHICH RESULTS IN RAISE OF METAL AT EDGE OF RECESS EXCEEDING 005 ABOVE SURROUNDING AREA.
- (18) THESE TENSILE VALUES APPLY TO ALLOY STEEL AND TITANIUM PARTS.
- (17) THESE TENSILE VALUES APPLY TO CRES PARTS. SEE NOTE 8.
- (19) DIAMETER DASH NUMBER 7 IS INACTIVE FOR DESIGN AFTER FEBRUARY 1977, USE 7B.
- (21) THIS STANDARD TAKES PRECEDENCE OVER DOCUMENTS REFERENCED HEREIN.
- (20) THREADS IN ACCORDANCE WITH AS8879; MAJOR DIAMETER SHALL BE .001 BELOW MINUMUM SHANK DIAMETER.
- (22) FOR A286 CRES, MAGNETIC PERMEABILITY SHALL BE LESS THAN 2.0 (AIR = 1.0) FOR A FIELD STRENGTH H = 200 OERSTEDS USING A MAGNETIC PERMEABILITY INDICATOR PER ASTM A 342/A 342M, TEST METHOD 3.
- (23) REFERENCED DOCUMENTS SHALL BE THE ISSUE IN EFFECT ON DATE OF INVITATIONS FOR
- (24) DIMENSIONING AND TOLERANCING PER ANSI Y14.5M-1982.
- (26) INTERMEDIATE OR LONGER LENGTHS MAY BE SPECIFIED BY THE USE OF WHOLE DASH NUMBERS ONLY.
- (25) TOLERANCES UNLESS OTHERWISE SPECIFIED: DECIMALS ‡ .030, ANGLES ‡ 1°.

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

## PROCUREMENT SPECIFICATION:

NAS4002, NAS4003, NAS4004 AS APPLICABLE, EXCEPT AS NOTED. TENSILE STRENGTH AND RECESS TORQUE VALUES AS TABULATED HEREIN. TENSILE FATIGUE LIFE TESTS APPLY TO TITANIUM ONLY. FATIGUE TESTING SHALL BE PER NAS621 EXCEPT FATIGUE TEST LOAD SHALL BE 33.3% OF NAS1581 TABULATED TENSILE LOAD FOR HIGH LOAD AND R = .25. COLD WORKING OF HEAD TO SHANK FILLET IS NOT REQUIRED ON ALLOY STEEL OR CRES BOLTS.

REVISION

12

NAS1581

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

TABLE IV - GRIP AND LENGTH DIMENSIONS (CONT'D)

GRIP EQUALS GRIP DASH NUMBER TIMES .0625 (CONVERTED TO DECIMAL PLACES PER 1982). LENGTH EQUALS GRIP LENGTH PLUS "T".

| GRIP DASH NO.   | , NOMINAL GRIP   |   LENGTH FOR DIA DASH NUMBERS LISTED +.015 |   LENGTH FOR DIA DASH NUMBERS LISTED +.015 |   LENGTH FOR DIA DASH NUMBERS LISTED +.015 |   LENGTH FOR DIA DASH NUMBERS LISTED +.015 | LENGTH FOR DIA DASH NUMBERS LISTED +.015   |   LENGTH FOR DIA DASH NUMBERS LISTED +.015 |   LENGTH FOR DIA DASH NUMBERS LISTED +.015 | LENGTH FOR DIA DASH NUMBERS LISTED +.015   | NOMINAL GRIP   |
|-----------------|------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|----------------|
| GRIP DASH NO.   | , NOMINAL GRIP   |                                      3     |                                      4     |                                      5     |                                      6     | 7B                                         |                                      8     |                                      9     | 10                                         | NOMINAL GRIP   |
| 50              | 3.125            |                                      3.488 |                                      3.528 |                                      3.626 |                                      3.719 | 3.800                                      |                                      3.893 |                                      4.006 | 4.106                                      | 3.125          |
| 52              | 3.250            |                                      3.613 |                                      3.653 |                                      3.751 |                                      3.844 | 3.925                                      |                                      4.018 |                                   4131     | 4.231                                      | 3.250          |
| 54              | 3.375            |                                      3.738 |                                      3.778 |                                      3.876 |                                      3.969 | 4.050                                      |                                      4.143 |                                      4.256 | 4.356                                      | 3.375          |

FIGURE 4 - OVERSIZE DIMENSIONS

## TABLE V - OVERSIZE DIMENSIONS

| 0156 OVERSIZE         | 0156 OVERSIZE   | 0156 OVERSIZE   | 0156 OVERSIZE   | 0156 OVERSIZE   | 0156 OVERSIZE   | .0312 OVERSIZE        |
|-----------------------|-----------------|-----------------|-----------------|-----------------|-----------------|-----------------------|
| OVERSIZE DIA DASH NO. |                 |                 |                 |                 | H REF (12)      |                       |
| OVERSIZE DIA DASH NO. | UNPLATED        | UNPLATED        | PLATED          | PLATED          | H REF (12)      |                       |
| OVERSIZE DIA DASH NO. | MAX             | MIN             | MAX             | MIN             | H REF (12)      |                       |
| 3(XX)                 | .2051           | .2046           | 2051            | .2041           | .043            | USE NAS1580(3 XX)Y    |
| 4(XX)                 | .2651           | .2646           | 2651            | .2641           | .057            | USE NAS1580()4(XX)Y   |
| 5(XX)                 | .3276           | 3271            | 3276            | 3266            | .065            | USE NAS158005 XX)     |
|                       | .3901           | 3896            | 3901            | 3891            | .075            | USE NAS1580(16 XX)Y   |
|                       | .4526           | 4521            | 4526            | .4516           | .095            | USE NAS1580( XX/      |
| 8(XX)                 | .5151           | .5146           | 5151            | .5141           | 103             | USE NAS1580(8 XX)Y    |
|                       | 5771            | .5766           | .5771           | 5761            | 113             |                       |
| 10( XX)X              | .6396           | .6391           | .6396           | .6386           | .123            | USE NAS1580(10( XX )Y |

- NOTES: (1) SEE SHEETS 1 THRU 5 FOR MATERIAL, FINISH, PROCUREMENT INFORMATION AND DIMENSIONS NOT SHOWN.
- (2) MINIMUM TENSILE LOADS FOR "X" CODED PARTS ARE 85% OF THE STANDARD LOADS TABULATED ON SHEET 1.
